name: GeoRota do Orvalho (PR 3) — Orvalho Boardwalk circular route
name_original: GeoRota do Orvalho (PR 3) — percurso circular dos Passadiços do Orvalho
region_intro: Hiking around Orvalho, Portugal, offers a variety of trails through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and traditional Portuguese villages. The region features a mix of forested areas and open terrain, providing diverse scenery for outdoor activities. Many routes incorporate boardwalk sections, offering accessible paths along water features. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making the area suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Orvalho The most popular hiking route is GeoRota do Orvalho (PR 3) — Orvalho Boardwalk circular route, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route features scenic boardwalks and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Passadiços do Orvalho loop from Orvalho, an easy 1.6 miles (2.6 km) path. This shorter loop provides a pleasant walk along the Orvalho boardwalks, suitable for a quick outing. Local hikers also love the Rio Zêzere loop from Janeiro de Cima, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through the Zêzere river valley, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Orvalho is defined by river valleys, boardwalk paths, and forested hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more strenuous hikes. The routes in Orvalho are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Orvalho's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Orvalho?
A: There are over 7 hiking routes in the Orvalho region, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging moderate hikes. None of the trails are classified as difficult, making the area accessible for many.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Orvalho?
A: Yes, Orvalho offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14704243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passadiços do Orvalho loop from Orvalho&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.6-mile (2.6 km) path that provides a pleasant walk along the boardwalks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Orvalho?
A: The hiking trails in Orvalho feature a diverse landscape, including rolling hills, scenic river valleys, and traditional Portuguese villages. You'll encounter a mix of forested areas and open terrain, with many routes incorporating accessible boardwalk sections along water features. Elevation changes are generally moderate.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Orvalho?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Orvalho are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2131149700/georota-do-orvalho-pr-3-orvalho-boardwalk-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GeoRota do Orvalho (PR 3) — Orvalho Boardwalk circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.5 miles (10.4 km) long and features boardwalks and varied terrain.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking in Orvalho?
A: While hiking in Orvalho, you can discover several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6966605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passadiços do Orvalho&lt;/a&gt; (boardwalks) themselves are a highlight, offering unique perspectives along the river. You might also encounter the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4343722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fraga de Água d'Alta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7576386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Luzia Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Orvalho?
A: The hiking routes in Orvalho are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained boardwalks, the varied scenery of river valleys and forests, and the overall accessibility of the trails for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Zêzere river?
A: Yes, the Zêzere river valley is a prominent feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21948196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Zêzere loop from Janeiro de Cima&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.7-mile (9.1 km) trail that leads you directly through this scenic river valley, offering beautiful waterside views.

Q: Are the trails around Orvalho suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the trails in Orvalho, particularly the easy and moderate ones with boardwalk sections, are suitable for families. The moderate elevation changes and well-defined paths make it a pleasant experience for children. The shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14704243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passadiços do Orvalho loop from Orvalho&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for a family outing.

Q: What is the typical duration for a hike in Orvalho?
A: Hiking durations in Orvalho vary depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, easy loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14704243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passadiços do Orvalho loop from Orvalho&lt;/a&gt; can take around an hour. Longer, moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2131149700/georota-do-orvalho-pr-3-orvalho-boardwalk-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GeoRota do Orvalho (PR 3)&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any trails starting from specific villages or towns near Orvalho?
A: Yes, several trails offer starting points from nearby villages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21948196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Zêzere loop&lt;/a&gt; begins from Janeiro de Cima, and there's also an easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21948205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Casal da Lapa&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to explore the charming surroundings of these communities.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Orvalho?
A: Orvalho offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming wildflowers or colorful foliage. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, while winter offers a quieter experience, though some paths might be damp.
tips:
Hidden at the base of the Orvalho Walkways, the Fraga de Água d’Alta Waterfall slides down 10 metres of rock, forming a small crystal-clear pool around it. 

It is a true natural refuge, where a refreshing dip becomes even more irresistible in the scorching summers of Beira Baixa. When the place is calm, the sensation of swimming under the waterfall becomes magical. It is no coincidence that this is considered one of the most beautiful waterfalls in Portugal. And the highest in the region!

In the heart of Beira Baixa, in the heart of the Naturtejo Geopark, lies one of the most stunning walkways in Portugal. The Orvalho Walkways, part of the PR3 Orvalho GeoRoute, take you along a route where nature and geology intertwine in magnificent settings.

Here, you can not only explore UNESCO-listed geomonuments, such as the imposing Fraga de Água d'Alta Waterfall and Cabeço do Mosqueiro, but also admire some of the region's most breathtaking and iconic landscapes.

The climb up the walkways to the Cabeço do Mosqueiro viewpoint can be challenging, but every step is worth it. Along the way, traces of the past peek out from the surrounding nature: a stretch of Roman road, old watermills and the Forno das Mouras. Take a break, take a deep breath and let yourself be amazed by the landscape that unfolds around you.

This final stretch of the Orvalho Walkways is even more magical at dusk, when the golden light of the sun caresses the slopes. At the top of the cliff, the viewpoint offers an unforgettable view over the Chapel of Nossa Senhora da Confiança and the vast landscape.
